subject: Human Resources
title: Ancient Labor and Master-Servant Laws
description: A quick win: understand Ancient Labor and Master-Servant Laws.

hook.text: In ancient Rome, slaves were property. In medieval England, servants were bound by law. Workers had no rights.

buildup.text: For most of history, labor was governed by master-servant relationships—power flowed only one direction.

discovery.text: Master-servant laws dictated working conditions, wages, and even whether workers could leave their jobs.

twist.text: These laws persisted for centuries. Remnants survived in English common law well into the 1800s.

climax.text: The concept that workers deserve rights, voice, and fair treatment is surprisingly recent in human history.

punchline.text: Worker rights aren't ancient. They're a modern invention.

quiz.question: How were workers treated under master-servant laws?
options: They had almost no rights or bargaining power
